introduction: *A weathered man looks up from his vegetable cart*
Fresh turnips... carrots... good for traveling.
Used to mine the deep veins. Now I grow things that see the sun.
*The man's eyes widen as he sees you*
Your fur... it's standing on end.
*He looks troubled*
You feel them too, don't you? The tremors in the stone.

options.text: Yes, I can feel something wrong underground.

introduction: I knew it. Your kind... you sense things we can't.
Been getting worse these past weeks.
Whatever's down there... it's waking up.
Name's Henrik. Been in Redstone all my life.
Twenty years working the deep veins.
Best miner this town ever had.
*His hands shake slightly*
That was before.
Not selling today. Just... keeping my hands busy.
Used to work with a pickaxe. Now it's turnips.
*He looks at his scarred hands*
Safer this way.
*Henrik's voice drops low*
Five years ago, we broke through into something old.
Deep in Red Hollow Mine. Past where any map showed tunnels.
Found a chamber. Walls smooth as glass.
And the ore... gods, that ore.
Glowed with its own light. Blue-white, like frozen lightning.
Warm to the touch. Hummed when you got close.
Foreman called it 'Aethel Ore' from some old book.
Said it was worth a fortune.
*His voice hardens*
We should have left it buried.

options.text: Why should you have left it?

introduction: Night after we opened that chamber, the tremors started.
Just small ones at first. Figured it was settling.
But they kept coming. Getting stronger.
And people started... changing.
Dreams. Terrible dreams of voices underground.
Some miners refused to leave the shaft.
Others walked toward the ruins on the hill.
*He shivers*
Like something was calling them.
Town took most of it. Locked in the mayor's vault.
Said they'd study it. Find out what it was.
*He laughs bitterly*
I sealed the chamber. Collapsed the tunnel.
Left mining for good that day.

options.text: Did the tremors stop after you sealed it?

introduction: Because I saw what it did to good men.
Because I felt it in my bones every night.
That hum. That pull.
*His eyes are distant*
And because of what I saw when I went to the ruins.

options.text: What did you see at the ruins?
Did sealing it stop the tremors?

introduction: *Henrik's hands start shaking violently*
I don't... I can't talk about the ruins.
*He turns away*
Ask someone else. Anyone else.
Just not me.
*Henrik's voice cracks*
You don't understand. The words won't come.
I try to remember and it's like... like something takes them.
Please. Don't ask again.

options.text: I'm sorry. I won't ask again.

introduction: *Henrik takes a shaky breath*
The mine, though. The old shaft.
That I can talk about.

options.text: That's alright. Take care of yourself.

introduction: They're back. The tremors.
Started again three weeks ago.
Worse than before. Much worse.
*He looks at you intently*
And I've been hearing things from the old shaft.

options.text: Sounds like a problem for the miners.

introduction: Red Hollow Mine. Eastern edge of town.
The entrance I sealed is past the main workings.
Marked with my old crew's sign - crossed picks.
*He hesitates*
But the noises... they're coming from inside.

options.text: Do you want me to check it out?

introduction: Scratching. Digging. And... voices.
Not human voices.
*He looks at his shaking hands*
I can't go back down there. I just can't.
But someone needs to check what's gotten in.
Would you... could you investigate?

options.text: I'll check it out for you.
What should I expect down there?
I'm not sure I'm ready for that.

introduction: Could be cave-ins. Gas pockets. Flooding.
Or... something worse.
Whatever's making those noises isn't natural.
Take weapons. Take light.
And if you feel that pull... that hum...
Get out. Don't try to resist it.
*Henrik's eyes show relief mixed with worry*
Thank you. Truly.
Red Hollow Mine, eastern edge. Look for the crossed picks.
Be careful down there.
*He hands you a worn mining lantern*
This kept me safe for twenty years. Maybe it'll help you too.

options.text: Tell me more about what to expect.

introduction: *Henrik nods slowly*
I understand. It's not your burden.
But if you change your mind...
I'll be here. Selling turnips.
*He returns to his cart*
The old shaft goes deep. Three levels, maybe four.
I sealed the main tunnel, but there are side passages.
Things could have dug in from below.
*He pauses*
If you find any of that glowing ore... don't touch it.
And if the tremors start while you're down there...
Get to the surface. Fast.

options.text: What if I find ore samples?

introduction: *Henrik looks troubled*
If you must take samples, use thick cloth.
Don't let it touch your skin.
And don't keep it long.
That stuff... it gets in your head.
*Henrik looks up as you approach*
You're back. And alive.
*He searches your face*
What did you find down there?

options.text: Kobolds had moved in. I cleared them out.

introduction: *Henrik lets out a long breath*
Kobolds. Just kobolds.
That's... that's good. Normal monsters.
Not... other things.
*He counts out coins*
Here. For your trouble.

options.text: I also found these. [Show ore samples]
Thank you. The shaft is clear now.

introduction: *Henrik recoils from the glowing fragments*
Gods... you brought them up.
*His hands shake*
That's Aethel Ore. Same as before.
Which means the chamber... it's still there.
The collapse didn't destroy it. Just buried it.

options.text: What should I do with these?

introduction: Get rid of them. Bury them. Throw them in the lake.
*He looks at you seriously*
Or... take them to someone who knows about old magic.
The Mayor has scholars. Mages.
They might know what this ore really is.

options.text: I'll keep them safe for now.

introduction: Aye. He should know.
He's been asking about the tremors.
These ore samples... they're proof.
Proof that whatever we found five years ago...
It's not finished with us yet.

options.text: Thank you for trusting me with this.

introduction: *Henrik returns to his vegetables*
Thank you for checking the shaft.
At least now I know what the noises were.
*He looks tired*
Turnips don't ask questions. Turnips don't glow.
Much better than mining.

options.text: Can I ask you something else?

introduction: What do you want to know?

options.text: Tell me more about the Aethel Ore.

introduction: *Henrik looks up as you approach*
You're going down there, aren't you?
Into those ruins. Through the mine tunnel you found.
*His hands shake slightly*
I sealed that chamber years ago. Tried to keep it buried.
But if you're going... you'll need to know what you're facing.

options.text: What should I know about the tunnel?
Do you have anything that could help?
I'm not going yet. Just checking in.

introduction: The tunnel goes deep. Past where any map shows.
You'll hit cave-ins. Support beams rotted through years ago.
*He counts on his fingers*
Watch for gas pockets - they'll make you dizzy, confused.
And the ore... if you see that blue glow...
*He trails off*
Just don't touch it with bare hands. It gets in your head.

options.text: Is there anything you can give me to help?

introduction: *Henrik looks uncomfortable*
The old tunnels... they connect to something older.
Natural caverns. And below those...
*He swallows hard*
Whatever those ruins were built on top of.
When we found the chamber, it wasn't just ore.
There were carvings. Symbols. Things that shouldn't exist.
*He meets your eyes*
The mine tunnel will take you past the outer defenses.
Straight into... whatever's down there.

options.text: That's exactly what I need to know.
Maybe I should use the front entrance instead.

introduction: *Henrik nods slowly*
Might be safer that way. More time to prepare.
You'll face everything the ruins throw at you.
But at least you'll see it coming.
*A grim smile*
Either way, you're braver than I ever was.
*Henrik reaches into his cart and pulls out a reinforced lantern*
This was my foreman's. Better than the one I gave you before.
*He hands it over - it's heavier, with thicker glass*
Burns brighter. Lasts longer.
And the light... it keeps some things at bay.
*He taps the metal frame*
Saved my life more than once down there.
Maybe it'll do the same for you.

options.text: Thank you, Henrik. I'll put it to good use.

introduction: *Henrik extends his weathered hand*
Come back safe. That's all I ask.
*His grip is surprisingly strong*
And if you can... end whatever's down there.
So no one else has to go through what I did.
*He releases your hand*
Good luck. You'll need it.

options.text: I'll do my best. See you when I return.

introduction: *Henrik looks up as you approach*
You're back. And you're alive.
*He searches your face*
Is it... is it over? Really over?

options.text: It's over. The cult is finished.
The portal is sealed. The ore chamber is destroyed.

introduction: *Henrik looks up as you approach, his weathered face breaking into a genuine smile*
You're back. And alive.
*He grips your shoulders firmly*
It's over, isn't it? Really over.
I can feel it. The tremors have stopped.
*His eyes glisten*
After all these years... the weight's finally lifting.
*He releases you and steps back*
There's so much I want to ask. So much I need to know.

options.text: How do you feel now that it's over?
The mine tunnel helped us bypass the outer defenses.
What should we do with the Aethel ore samples?
Will you reopen the mine now?
I brought your lantern back. Do you want it returned?
That's all I wanted to share.

introduction: *He sits down on his cart, looking out over the square*
Lighter. Free.
For five years, I've carried the guilt of what we unleashed.
*His hands, usually shaking, are steady*
Every tremor reminded me. Every disappearance was my fault.
I sealed that chamber, but I knew it wasn't enough.
*He looks at you*
You did what I couldn't. What I was too afraid to do.
You went down there and ended it.
Thank you. Truly.
*Henrik's eyes widen*
You found the connection? The tunnel from the chamber?
*He nods slowly*
I always suspected the cult knew about it.
That's why they wanted the Aethel ore so badly.
The chamber wasn't just a source of power. It was a backdoor.
*A grim smile*
Smart thinking, using it. Probably saved lives.
The cultists never expected an attack from below.
*He looks troubled*
The Aethel ore. Even the name tastes like poison now.
*He thinks carefully*
Part of me says destroy it. Melt it down. Bury it in the lake.
But another part...
*He gestures to the town*
Knowledge preserved us before. The Mayor's scholars.
If we destroy it, we forget. And forgotten dangers return.
*His voice hardens*
Seal it away. Deep. With warnings.
Let our grandchildren know what we faced.
So they never dig that deep again.
*Henrik looks at his weathered hands*
I've thought about it. Every day since the tremors stopped.
*He pauses*
The upper levels are safe. Good copper veins. Some silver.
We could work those. Bring jobs back to Redstone.
*His voice drops*
But the deep shaft? The chamber?
That stays sealed. Forever.
*He looks up*
I'll oversee it personally. New seals. Proper wards.
And if anyone asks why we don't dig deeper...
*A grim smile*
I'll tell them about turnips. How they grow toward the sun.
Not everything valuable lies in the dark.
*Henrik looks at the reinforced lantern you're holding*
My foreman's lantern. You kept it safe.
*He reaches out, then hesitates*
That lantern saw me through twenty years underground.
It's probably worth more to me than anything else I own.
*He meets your eyes*
But you're the one who took it into the deepest dark.
You're the one who came back.
The choice is yours. Keep it, or return it.

options.text: It's yours, Henrik. You should have it back.
If you're sure... I'd like to keep it. As a reminder.

introduction: *Henrik takes the lantern carefully, his hands trembling*
Thank you. You don't know what this means.
*He holds it close*
This light guided me through the darkest places.
Now it's home. Where it belongs.
*He looks up with tears in his eyes*
You're a good soul. The kind this town needs.
*He reaches into his coin purse*
Take this. For bringing back more than just a lantern.
*Henrik nods with understanding*
Then keep it. Use it well.
*A sad but genuine smile*
Maybe it's fitting. That lantern was meant for the depths.
For those brave enough to face what lies below.
You've earned it. More than I ever did.
*He places his hand on your shoulder*
Just... bring it back to the surface sometimes.
Let it see the sun. Remember there's light above the dark.
*Henrik stands and extends his hand*
You've given me something I thought I'd lost forever.
Peace. A chance to make things right.
*He pulls out a heavy coin purse*
This is from the town. For clearing the mine route.
For ending the nightmare we started five years ago.
*He presses it into your hands*
And this...
*He pulls out a small, glowing crystal - carefully wrapped in cloth*
A piece of Aethel ore. Properly sealed.
Not as a weapon. As a reminder.
That even the deepest darkness can be overcome.
*Henrik sits down heavily on his cart*
Gods. I never thought...
*He wipes his eyes*
All these years. That weight on my shoulders.
Knowing what I left down there. What could happen.
*He looks up at you*
You did what I couldn't. What I was too afraid to do.
Thank you. From the bottom of my heart.

options.text: It was an honor to help.
